description: Validate Supabase pgvector version supports required embedding dimensions (1024) before setup
steps: Pipeline runs `validate-environment` command
System checks pgvector extension version: SELECT extversion FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'vector'
System queries pgvector capabilities for max dimensions on HNSW/IVFFlat indexes
System verifies if 1024 dimensions are supported for chosen index type (baai/bge-m3 via OpenRouter)
IF NOT SUPPORTED (unlikely - 1024 << 2000 limit): System halts and displays error
IF SUPPORTED: System confirms HNSW index can be created for 1024-dimensional embeddings
System validates OpenRouter API key is configured (OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var)
System logs validation results: pgvector version, index support, OpenRouter connectivity

description: Initialize Supabase schema with pgvector extension, documents table, chunks table with 1024-dim embeddings, and HNSW similarity search index
steps: Pipeline runs `setup-database` command against Supabase project
System enables pgvector extension via `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector`

System creates `documents` table with columns: id (UUID PK), internal_id (TEXT UNIQUE), refid (TEXT UNIQUE), refname (TEXT), sygnatura (TEXT), title (TEXT), date_announcement (DATE), date_publication (DATE), keywords (TEXT[]), source_url (TEXT), body_text (TEXT), meta_json (JSONB), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)

System creates `chunks` table with columns: id (UUID PK), document_id (UUID FK references documents.id ON DELETE CASCADE), chunk_index (INT), chunk_text (TEXT), embedding (VECTOR(1024)), token_count (INT), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)
System creates unique index on chunks(document_id, chunk_index)

System creates HNSW index on chunks.embedding for fast cosine similarity search: CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_embedding_hnsw ON chunks USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64)

System creates `sync_metadata` table with columns: id (UUID PK), last_sync_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), status (TEXT), checkpoint_data (JSONB), total_documents (INT), total_chunks (INT), total_tokens_used (BIGINT), total_cost_usd (DECIMAL), embedding_model (TEXT DEFAULT 'baai/bge-m3'), embedding_dimensions (INT DEFAULT 1024), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)

System creates RPC function `match_chunks(query_embedding VECTOR(1024), match_threshold FLOAT, match_count INT)` that returns chunks joined with document metadata ordered by cosine similarity using HNSW index
System confirms all tables and indexes created successfully: documents table, chunks table with HNSW index, sync_metadata table, match_chunks RPC function

description: Setup is idempotent — running setup-database a second time does not fail or duplicate objects
steps: Database already has all tables, indexes, and functions from a previous setup run
Pipeline runs `setup-database` again
All CREATE statements use IF NOT EXISTS or CREATE OR REPLACE
System completes without errors
Existing data in tables is not affected

description: Initial bulk fetch discovers all UODO document IDs across the full time range with pagination
steps: Pipeline runs `fetch-documents --mode bulk`
System calls GET /documents/search/PublicDocument/,/publicator_subtype:eq:uodo?from=0&count=100&order=-id&fields=id,refid,refname,title,dates
API returns first 100 document objects
System detects that result count equals 100 (page is full), increments offset to 100
System calls next page: ?from=100&count=100&order=-id&fields=id,refid,refname,title,dates
System continues paginating until a page returns fewer than 100 results
System stores all discovered document references (internal_id, refid, refname, title, dates) in a local manifest or queue
System logs total count: 'Discovered N documents from UODO API'

description: Incremental fetch discovers only new documents since last sync timestamp
steps: Pipeline runs `fetch-documents --mode incremental`
System reads last sync timestamp from `sync_metadata` table in Supabase (e.g., 2025-11-01)
System constructs timespan as `2025-11-01,` (from last sync to now)
System calls GET /documents/search/PublicDocument/2025-11-01,/publicator_subtype:eq:uodo?from=0&count=100&order=-id&fields=id,refid,refname,title,dates
System paginates through all results
System filters out documents whose refid already exists in the `documents` table
System logs: 'Found M new documents since 2025-11-01'

description: Download body text, metadata, title, and dates for a single document via refpath
steps: Pipeline processes document with refid 'urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9'
System constructs refpath by appending ':0' for body content
System calls GET /documents/public/items/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9:0/body.txt
API returns plain text body of the decision
System calls GET /documents/public/items/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9/meta.json
API returns metadata JSON
System calls GET /documents/public/items/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9/title.json
API returns title and subtitle JSON
System calls GET /documents/public/items/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9/dates.json
API returns dates JSON (announcement, publication, etc.)
System assembles a complete document record with body_text, metadata, title, dates
System constructs source_url as 'https://orzeczenia.uodo.gov.pl/orzeczenie/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:dkn_5131_9'

description: Batch fetch content for multiple documents with rate limiting and concurrency control
steps: Pipeline has a queue of 150 documents to fetch content for
System processes documents in parallel with concurrency limit of 5
System enforces minimum 200ms delay between requests to avoid overwhelming UODO API
System tracks progress: 'Fetching content: 47/150 documents processed'
System retries failed requests up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s)
System logs total time and success/failure counts upon completion
System stores successfully fetched documents and queues failed ones for retry

description: Split a standard-length document into overlapping chunks for embedding
steps: Pipeline receives a document with ~3000 tokens of body text
System splits text into chunks targeting 500-1000 tokens per chunk
System applies ~100 token overlap between consecutive chunks
System splits on paragraph boundaries when possible, falling back to sentence boundaries
Each chunk retains metadata: document_id, refid, sygnatura, title, date, chunk_index
System produces 4-5 chunks for this document
System logs: 'Document DKN.5131.9.2025 split into 5 chunks (avg 650 tokens)'

description: Chunk a very long document (>10000 tokens) maintaining coherent sections
steps: Pipeline receives a document with ~15000 tokens of body text
System detects document exceeds typical length
System first attempts to split on section/heading boundaries if present
System then applies token-based chunking within sections that exceed 1000 tokens
System ensures overlap between all consecutive chunks
System produces ~18-20 chunks
System logs: 'Long document DKN.5131.4.2025 split into 20 chunks'

description: Batch generate embeddings for document chunks using OpenRouter baai/bge-m3 (1024 dimensions)
steps: Pipeline has 50 chunks ready for embedding
System batches chunks into groups of up to 20 chunks per batch
System calls OpenRouter embeddings API with model='baai/bge-m3' via OpenAI-compatible SDK
API endpoint: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/embeddings
API returns 1024-dimensional vectors for each chunk in the batch
System associates each embedding vector with its corresponding chunk
System repeats for remaining batches
System logs: 'Generated embeddings for 50 chunks in 3 batches, total tokens: 32000, estimated cost: $0.0003' (13x cheaper than OpenAI text-embedding-3-large)

description: Insert new document with its chunks and embeddings into Supabase
steps: Pipeline has a fully processed document with 5 chunks and their embeddings
System inserts a row into `documents` table with metadata (refid, sygnatura, title, dates, keywords, body_text, source_url)
System receives the generated document UUID
System batch-inserts 5 rows into `chunks` table, each with document_id FK, chunk_index, chunk_text, embedding vector, token_count
System verifies row counts: 1 document + 5 chunks inserted
System logs: 'Ingested document DKN.5131.9.2025 with 5 chunks into Supabase'

description: Upsert a re-ingested document replacing old chunks with new ones
steps: Pipeline processes a document that was previously ingested (refid already exists in documents table)
System detects existing document via refid lookup
System updates the `documents` row with new metadata and body_text, sets updated_at = NOW()
System deletes all existing chunks for this document_id (CASCADE or explicit DELETE)
System inserts new chunks with fresh embeddings
System verifies updated document has correct chunk count
System logs: 'Updated document DKN.5131.9.2025: replaced 4 old chunks with 5 new chunks'

description: Full incremental sync pipeline: discover new documents, fetch content, chunk, embed, and ingest
steps: Pipeline runs `sync` (scheduled daily or triggered manually)
Step 1: System runs fetch-documents in incremental mode, discovers 3 new documents
Step 2: System runs fetch-content for each new document, downloads body and metadata
Step 3: System runs chunk-documents on each fetched document
Step 4: System runs generate-embeddings for all new chunks (batch)
Step 5: System runs ingest-to-supabase for each document with its chunks and embeddings
Step 6: System updates sync_metadata.last_sync_at to current timestamp
System logs: 'Sync complete: 3 new documents, 14 chunks, 0 errors'

description: Sync skips already-ingested documents by comparing refids
steps: Pipeline runs incremental sync
fetch-documents returns 10 document references from API
System queries Supabase for existing refids
7 of 10 documents already exist in the database
System filters to only 3 new documents for processing
System proceeds with fetch-content, chunk, embed, ingest for only the 3 new documents
System logs: 'Skipped 7 already-ingested documents, processing 3 new'

description: Bulk sync mode processes all historical documents for initial database population
steps: Pipeline runs `sync --mode bulk` for initial database population
System runs fetch-documents in bulk mode, discovers all documents from UODO API (e.g., 554 total)
System queries Supabase for existing refids to skip already-ingested documents
System filters to only new documents that need processing
For each new document: fetch content, chunk, generate embeddings, ingest to database
System tracks progress: 'Processing document 47/554...'
System reports errors for failed documents but continues processing remaining documents
System updates sync_metadata.last_sync_at to current timestamp after completion
System logs final summary: 'Bulk sync complete: 554 documents discovered, 500 new documents processed, 48 errors, elapsed time: 8.5 hours'

description: Handle 404 when document content is not found at the given refpath
steps: System calls GET /documents/public/items/urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:nonexistent:0/body.txt
API returns 404 with message: 'Nie znaleziono zdarzenia dla wybranego refpath'
System logs warning: 'Document not found: urn:ndoc:gov:pl:uodo:2025:nonexistent (404)'
System marks document as failed in the processing queue with reason 'NOT_FOUND'
System continues processing remaining documents without interruption
Failed document is included in the sync summary report

description: Handle 415 unsupported media type when requesting body in unavailable format
steps: System calls GET /documents/public/items/{refpath}:0/body.txt
API returns 415 with message: 'Nieobsługiwany typ mediów txt dla wybranego refpath'
System logs warning: 'Unsupported format for {refpath}: 415'
System falls back to requesting body.html instead
If fallback succeeds, system strips HTML tags to extract plain text
If fallback also fails, system marks document as failed with reason 'UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT'

description: Handle UODO API timeout or network failure with retry and exponential backoff
steps: System calls GET /documents/search/PublicDocument/... with 30s timeout
Request times out (ETIMEDOUT) or network error occurs (ECONNRESET)
System logs: 'UODO API request failed: ETIMEDOUT. Retrying in 1s (attempt 1/3)'
System waits 1 second and retries
Second attempt also fails
System logs: 'Retrying in 2s (attempt 2/3)'
Third attempt succeeds, pipeline continues normally
If all 3 retries fail, system logs error and marks the page as failed for later retry

description: Handle Supabase connection failure during ingestion with transaction rollback
steps: System begins transaction to insert document and chunks
System successfully inserts document row
Supabase connection drops during chunk insertion (3 of 5 chunks inserted)
Transaction is automatically rolled back — no partial data persists
System logs: 'Supabase ingestion failed for DKN.5131.9.2025: connection lost. Transaction rolled back.'
System queues the document for retry in next sync cycle
System continues processing other documents

description: Sync resumes from checkpoint after partial failure mid-pipeline
steps: Sync starts processing 10 new documents
Documents 1-6 are fully processed and ingested successfully
Document 7 fails during embedding generation (OpenAI outage)
System logs error and records checkpoint: 'Sync checkpoint: 6/10 documents complete, failed at document 7'
System saves pipeline state (processed doc IDs, pending doc IDs) to sync_metadata
When sync is re-triggered, system reads checkpoint
System resumes from document 7, skipping already-ingested documents 1-6
System completes remaining 4 documents

description: Empty or very short documents (< 50 tokens) are stored as a single chunk without splitting
steps: Pipeline receives a document with body text of only 30 tokens
Chunking logic detects text is below minimum chunk threshold (50 tokens)
System creates a single chunk containing the full document text
Chunk metadata includes chunk_index=0 and the full document metadata
System logs: 'Document DKN.5131.2.2025 too short for splitting, stored as single chunk (30 tokens)'

description: Chunk overlap preserves sentence boundaries — never splits mid-sentence
steps: Pipeline processes a document with paragraphs of varying length
System reaches a chunk boundary at approximately 800 tokens
Token boundary falls in the middle of a sentence
System extends the chunk to include the complete sentence
Next chunk starts with ~100 tokens of overlap from the previous chunk's end
Overlap region also respects sentence boundaries
No chunk contains a truncated sentence at its start or end

description: Data integrity verification after batch ingestion confirms counts and embedding dimensions
steps: Pipeline completes ingesting 10 documents with 48 total chunks
System runs verification query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM documents WHERE created_at > (sync start time)
Result is 10 — matches expected document count
System runs verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM chunks WHERE document_id IN (newly inserted document IDs)
Result is 48 — matches expected chunk count
System verifies no NULL embeddings: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM chunks WHERE embedding IS NULL AND document_id IN (...)
Result is 0 — all chunks have embeddings
System logs: 'Integrity check passed: 10 documents, 48 chunks, 0 null embeddings'

description: Pagination correctly handles edge case where total document count is an exact multiple of page size
steps: UODO API has exactly 200 documents matching the filter
System fetches page 1 (from=0, count=100): receives 100 results
System detects full page, fetches page 2 (from=100, count=100): receives 100 results
System detects full page, fetches page 3 (from=200, count=100): receives 0 results (empty array)
System detects empty page and stops pagination
Total discovered documents: 200
System does not enter infinite loop or make unnecessary requests

description: Concurrent sync runs are prevented by a database-level lock
steps: Sync process A starts and acquires advisory lock in sync_metadata (status='running')
Sync process B is triggered (e.g., duplicate cron invocation)
Process B checks sync_metadata and finds status='running' with recent heartbeat
Process B logs: 'Another sync is already in progress (started at ...). Skipping.'
Process B exits cleanly without processing any documents
Process A completes normally and releases the lock (status='idle')
